title: Gooey Malaysian Soya Milk Char Kway Teow
description: A savory, gooey, and nostalgic Malaysian dish featuring soya milk, shio-cure, and lacto-vegetarian-friendly ingredients.

ingredients.name: Soya Milk
ingredients.notes: use a mixture of soya milk and water for a creamy sauce

ingredients.name: Shio-Cured Tofu
ingredients.notes: cured in a mixture of sugar, salt, and rice vinegar

instructions: Heat 1 tablespoon of vegetable oil in a wok or large skillet over medium-high heat.
Add the chopped shallots and cook until fragrant, about 2 minutes.
Add the chopped garlic and cook for another minute, until fragrant.
Add the ground turmeric, cinnamon, and white pepper, and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Add the shredded tofu and cook until heated through.
Add the noodles and cook until heated through.
Pour in the soya milk mixture and stir everything together.
Bring the mixture to a simmer and cook until the sauce has thickened slightly, about 2-3 minutes.
Taste and adjust the seasoning as needed.
Transfer the char kway teow to a serving platter and garnish with chopped scallions and cilantro.
